role: Alleged MOIS officer; operated from Iranian Embassy in Vienna
bio: Hamid Naghashan is alleged in Belgian and Austrian intelligence reporting to have operated as a [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) officer from the [Iranian Embassy in Vienna](/orgs/iranian-embassy-vienna). He has been named in connection with the network of Iranian intelligence activity in Europe that included [Assadollah Assadi](/people/assadollah-assadi), who served as MOIS station chief in Vienna from 2014 to 2018 and was convicted in Belgium for the [2018 Villepinte bomb plot](/events/villepinte-bomb-plot-2018). Naghashan maintains a low public profile and details remain limited to intelligence and law-enforcement reporting.
editorial_notes: Extremely low-profile figure. No independent sourcing found in web searches as of April 2026. Named in Belgian/Austrian intelligence context around the Assadi case per the ingestion brief, but no publicly available documents confirm this. All connections are alleged. Keep minimal until better sourcing emerges.
